scopeNote: SYBEORG only. Radi / House of Meroe targets, drafts, files, accounts, and campaigns excluded. No outbound without Stark approval.

sources: DuckDuckGo HTML result snippets where available
repo de-duplication against mockups.json, master-send-board.json, and recent daily batches

candidateScores.business: D and L Plumbing and Heating

leads.gmbSignals: DuckDuckGo result showed a request-an-estimate page; direct fetch returned HTTP 404 with title SITE NOT FOUND. Broader Google rating not verified in this pass.
leads.contactRoute: manual research / phone before any email; no public email verified
verifiedEvidence: Direct fetch of request-an-estimate URL returned HTTP 404.
Fetched title was SITE NOT FOUND.
leads.diagnosis: The public estimate path appears broken, so high-intent visitors may hit a dead end before requesting service.
leads.bestHook: Helpful alert: request-an-estimate page returned SITE NOT FOUND; sample shows a clean replacement path.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific, visible public bug in a high-intent home-service vertical.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: The broken estimate path is visual and concrete; a sample replacement strengthens the helpful-alert note.
leads.designDifferentiation: Light blue/cream estimate-path repair concept with route cards; not dark hero/right-form/cards/reviews.
leads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed after fixing internal CTA wording: credible light estimate-path concept; clear sample framing; phone marked pending confirmation rather than using an unverified number; no fake proof or generic testimonial blocks.
leads.subject: quick estimate-page note for Southwest Iowa Plumbing
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Southwest Iowa Plumbing and Heating team — I found a public request-an-estimate page for your business that returned a SITE NOT FOUND page during my check. If that page is still connected anywhere customers click, it could block quote requests before they reach you.

I made a no-obligation sample showing how that first estimate path could look once the real phone, services, and photos are confirmed: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/southwest-iowa-plumbing-heating/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: Overpass/OSM public listing surfaced auto repair category, Cedar Rapids-area phone +1 319-377-7060 and website https://autoevolution.us/. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: phone/manual research; no public email found in fetch
verifiedEvidence: Direct fetch of https://autoevolution.us/ returned HTTP 406.
Fetched page title was Not Acceptable!.
leads.diagnosis: The site may block some visitors or automated/mobile contexts; a call-first repair page could preserve leads if the public path fails.
leads.bestHook: Helpful alert: site returned Not Acceptable during check; sample shows a more reliable first screen.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific technical issue plus public phone in a high-intent auto-repair category.
leads.recommendedChannel: manual phone script or researched email only
leads.mockupDecisionReason: A service-bay sample makes the public-path issue easy to understand without criticizing their business.
leads.designDifferentiation: Service-bay/ticket-board composition; avoids stock mechanic hero, fake certifications, fake reviews, and right-side quote form.
leads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed after changing badge from ASE to AE to avoid certification ambiguity; credible service-bay/ticket-board concept; layout intact; phone CTA visible; no fake reviews/badges/warranties.
leads.subject: quick website access note for Automotive Service Evolution
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Automotive Service Evolution team — I checked the public site I found for ASE and it returned a “Not Acceptable” page during my pass. It may be harmless, but if some visitors hit the same screen, they would not see a repair path or phone CTA.

I made a no-obligation sample of a cleaner first screen using only public info I could verify: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/automotive-service-evolution/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: OSM public listing surfaced Cedar Rapids phone +1-319-373-7020 and website. Direct fetch found mailto promuf3@gmail.com and promuffler3@gmail.com; Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email mailto plus phone; email source should be rechecked in browser before send
verifiedEvidence: Official page loaded HTTP 200 with title Muffler Experts | Cedar Rapids, IA | Professional Muffler Inc.
Fetched HTML contained real mailto addresses and also the placeholder string your@email.com.
Page H1 text appeared as dMuffler & Brake Repair on Collins Rd in Cedar Rapids, IA in raw fetch.
leads.diagnosis: The site works, but a placeholder email string and awkward fetched H1 suggest a small public QA issue; first touch should be a helpful note, not a redesign pitch.
leads.bestHook: Helpful QA alert: placeholder email string appears in page HTML; offer quick cleanup/service-page polish.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: They have a reachable public email and the issue is precise/non-insulting.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; the current site is functional and a generic auto mockup would weaken the specific QA-alert angle.
leads.subject: quick website QA note for Professional Muffler
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Professional Muffler team — I was looking at the Collins Road muffler/brake page and noticed a small public QA item: the page HTML includes a placeholder-looking email string alongside the real contact emails. Your site is already live, so this is not a redesign pitch — just a quick cleanup/service-page polish idea.

If useful, I can send over the exact spot and a cleaner version of that first screen. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: Nominatim/OSM public listing surfaced roofing category, Davenport address, phone +1 563-549-9544, website, and email kendrick@burrageroofing.com. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing; official site has forms/phone; email should be source-verified before send
leads.emailSource: OSM public listing; direct official-site fetch did not expose same mailto in first pass
verifiedEvidence: Official site loaded HTTP 200 with title #1 Roofing Davenport IA Company.
Direct fetch found storm, roof repair, hail, residential, commercial, estimate, and review keywords.
Public listing supplied phone and email.
unsafeOrUnverifiedClaims: insurance claim expertise beyond verified site wording
leads.diagnosis: The site is functional; the stronger close is a focused storm/roof-repair landing page for paid search/seasonal traffic, not a homepage rebuild.
leads.bestHook: Additive offer: one Davenport storm/roof-repair landing page that can support ads and lead tracking.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Roofing is high-ticket, public contact route exists, and the offer does not insult their current site.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; a high-quality roofing page needs deeper proof/photos and page audit to avoid becoming generic.
leads.subject: quick storm-page idea for Burrage Roofing
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Burrage Roofing team — I came across your Davenport roofing site while researching storm/roof-repair pages. Your site already loads, so I would not lead with a generic redesign pitch. The clearer opportunity looks like one focused roof-repair/storm-inspection landing page that could support seasonal searches or ads.

If useful, I can mock up a focused page using verified details from your site/listing and leave room for your real photos/proof. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: DuckDuckGo result and direct fetch verified official site; direct fetch found contact form, 4 tel links, emergency/financing/water heater/commercial/residential keywords. Google rating not verified.
leads.contactRoute: contact form or phone/manual; no public email found in fetch
verifiedEvidence: Official site loaded HTTP 200 with title Plumber | Breese Plumbing & Heating LLC | Iowa City, IA.
Direct fetch found a form, four tel links, and water heater/emergency/financing keywords.
No public email found in fetched HTML.
leads.diagnosis: The site is functional and has phone/form paths, but the opportunity is local-profile/contact conversion: make emergency/water-heater visitors land on a simpler phone-first page and confirm GMB fields.
leads.bestHook: Profile/contact-path audit plus optional water-heater/emergency landing page after confirmation.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Local plumbing/HVAC is high-intent and the angle is additive, but contact route needs manual approval because email was not found.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; existing site is functional and the best visual angle requires verified GMB/review/photo proof first.
leads.subject: quick Iowa City plumbing page idea
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Breese Plumbing and Heating team — I found your Iowa City plumbing/HVAC site while checking local service pages. The site is live and has contact paths, so I would not lead with a full redesign pitch. The more useful opportunity looks like a tighter emergency/water-heater page and a quick local-profile/contact-path audit so search visitors know exactly what to tap next.

If useful, I can put together a focused sample using only verified details from your site. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.
